original: Saint Dionysius elected as Pope, succeeding Sixtus II
Godfrey of Bouillon is elected the first Defender of the Holy Sepulchre of the Kingdom of Jerusalem during the First Crusade
French Prince Louis begins siege of Dover Castle in Kent, England - abandoned that October. Influential in denying Louis the English throne.
English defeat the Scots at the Battle of Falkirk
King Phillip the Fair, orders expulsion of Jews out of France
Battle of St. Jakob an der Sihl: Army of the Old Swiss Confederacy defeats an allied force of Zurich and Habsburg troops at the gates of Zurich; without means to lay siege to the city, they withdraw

Battle of Lochmaben Fair - a 500-man raiding party led by Alexander Stewart, Duke of Albany and James Douglas, 9th Earl of Douglas are defeated by Scots forces loyal to Albany's brother James III of Scotland; Douglas is captured
First Congress of Vienna settles issues between Poland and the Holy Roman Empire, demonstrating the Habsburgs' growing influence
Christians captured in Tunis in uprising against Admiral Barbarossa
2nd English colony forms on Roanoke Island off North Carolina
10,000 Jews of Polannoe murdered in Chmielnick massacre during Khmelnytsky Uprising
City of Albany, NY chartered
Battle at Aghrim: English/Dutch army beats France
Diamonds found in Minas Geras, Brazil
Spain signs Treaty of Vienna
Ottoman Empire defeats Austria at Crocyka (Krotzka) in Serbia and then successfully lays siege to Belgrade
Cleveland, Ohio, founded by General Moses Cleaveland. Originally called 'Cleaveland', the public adopted the current name after a newspaper editor noticed the name was too long to fit on the page.
Underarm slow right arm bowler V E Walker takes 10-74 in 1st innings for an All-England Cricket XI v Surrey CCC at The Oval in London
Katharine Lee Bates writes "America the Beautiful" in Colorado
Crew of Belgian RV Belgica sees first sunrise in 1,600 hours during its first expedition to endure Antarctic winter
British House of Lords, in its role as court, rules trade unions can be sued for actions of its members - in Taff Vale Case
Serbia reactivates diplomatic relations with Montenegro
Philadelphia Athletic's Weldon Henley no-hits St Louis Browns, 6-0
In the face of ever-increasing German naval power, the British Admiralty decides to recall British warships from the Mediterranean and base them in the North Sea
V Summer Olympic Games close at Stockholm Olympiastadion, Sweden
A bomb explodes during a Preparedness Day parade in San Francisco killing 10
British bomb German lines at Ypres, 4,250,000 grenades
Lightning kills 504 sheep in Utah's Wasatch National Park
De Falla & Massine's "Three-cornered Hat" premieres in London
Cards enter 1st place, marks 1st time both St Louis teams are on top
105°F (41°C), Waterbury, Connecticut (state record)
108°F (42°C), Troy, NY (state record)
Cincinnati Reds right fielder Curt Walker ties National League record of 2 triples in an inning in a 13-1 rout of the Boston Braves
Caterina Jarboro sings "Aida" at the New York Hippodrome, becoming the first Black female opera singer to perform in the United States
Lester Walton appointed US minister to Liberia
Philadelphia Phillies outfielder Johnny Moore hits 3 straight home runs in a 16-4 rout of the Pittsburgh Pirates
Jane Bolin becomes the 1st African American female judge in New York
4th Soviet army forms with 80 tanks
Gasoline rationing using coupons begins
Transport #9 departs Drancy, France with 996 French Jews sent to Auschwitz Concentration camp; 7 survive until the war's end
Soviets set up Polish Committee of National Liberation
-8°F (-22°C), Charlotte Pass, NSW (Australian record low temperature)
Frank Worrell completes 261 v England at Trent Bridge
King Leopold returns to Belgium after 6 years in exile
General Francisco Craveiro Lopes appointed President of Portugal
Poland adopts Communist-imposed Constitution
US Virgin Islands adopt constitution (Revised Organic Act)
WTHI TV channel 10 in Terre Haute, IN (CBS) begins broadcasting
Philadelphia Phillies beat the St. Louis Cardinals, 5-3 to complete a team record 11-game winning streak; longest win-streak since 1892
US performs atmospheric nuclear Test at Bikini Island
Natural gas found at Slochteren, Groningen, Netherlands
Cuba nationalizes all US-owned sugar factories
WBNB TV channel 10 in Charlotte Amaile, Virgin Islands (CBS) begins broadcasting
1st US Venus probe, Mariner 1, fails at lift-off
Chicago White Sox outfielder Floyd Robinson goes 6 for 6 (all singles) as WS defeat Boston Red Sox, 7-3 at Fenway Park
Sarawak achieves independence from British colonial rule
1st major appearance by Vanilla Fudge (Village Theater NYC)
Atlanta Braves establish MLB record for number of pitchers called upon in one inning; 5 used in 9th inning of 5-4 defeat to St. Louis Cardinals
Sir John Newsome recommends public schools should take 50% of their intake from the state school system
USSR launches Sputnik 50 & Molniya 1-12 communications satellite
Sudanese military counter-coup under premier Numeiry
10.84" (27.53 cm) of rainfall, Fort Ripley, Mn (state 24-hr record)
2 Catholics are abducted, beaten, and shot dead in a Loyalist area of Belfast
Venera 8 makes soft landing on Venus
Academic Text Processing Service forms in Seattle
Australian pilot Dick Smith makes first solo helicopter flight around the world
MLB California Angels outfielder Brian Downing's error ends his record streak at 244 games
US House of Representatives impeaches Judge Harry E. Claiborne for tax evasion
Said Aouita of Morocco sets 5k record (12:58.39) in Rome
Soyuz TM-3 launches with three cosmonauts (one Syrian)
US begins escorting reflagged Kuwaiti tankers in Persian Gulf
500 US scientists pledge to boycott Pentagon germ-warfare research
Kristin Huxhold, 18, of Missouri, crowned America's Junior Miss
Soyuz TM-15 launches
Soyuz TM-17 lands
23rd & last part of Comet Shoemaker-Levy hit Jupiter (since July 16th)
Mariners play Red Sox as home team at Fenway, as Kingdome is repaired
Military coup in Gambia: President Dawda Jawara flees
MLB pitcher Doc Gooden admits to entering Betty Ford Center in Rancho Mirage, California, for substance abuse rehabilitation
William Sigei runs world record 10k (26:52.53)
Space shuttle STS-70 (Discovery 20) lands
Susan Smith found guilty of drowning her two children in South Carolina
"One Piece," world's best-selling manga and comic series, written and illustrated by Eiichiro Oda, first appears serialized in "Weekly Shōnen Jump" in Japan
Fire breaks out at Palais de Chaillot in Paris during renovations, damaging the Museum of Monuments and Museum of Cinema
The first shipments of oil produced from Kazakhstan's Tengiz field arrive at terminals on the Black Sea for subsequent export through the Bosphoros Strait
The second Blue Water Bridge opens between Port Huron, Michigan and Sarnia, Ontario
Israel assassinates Salah Shahade, the Commander-in-Chief of Hamas's military arm, the Izz ad-Din al-Qassam Brigades, along with 14 civilians

Members of  US 101st Airborne, aided by Special Forces, attack a compound in Mosul, Iraq, killing Uday Hussein and Qusay Hussein, sons of Saddam Hussein, along with Mustapha Hussein, Qusay's 14-year old son, and a bodyguard
Jean Charles de Menezes is killed by police as the hunt begins for the London Bombers. See 7 July 2005 London bombings and 21 July 2005 London bombings
Norway is the victim of twin terror attacks, a bomb blast targeting government buildings in central Oslo and a massacre at a youth camp on the island of Utøya
At least 77 people are killed by torrential rain in Beijing, China
Car bombs kills 20 people and injures 80 in Madaen and Najaf, Iraq
Hashim Amla smashes 311 not out in South Africa's inning and 12 run 1st Test win over England at The Oval; first South African to score a triple-century in Test cricket
Pranab Mukherjee is elected 13th President of India
89 people are killed and 500 are injured by earthquakes in Gansu province, China
Both the United Arab Emirates and the USA announce aid packages to besieged Gaza
European Union claims that Israel "has the right to defend itself," but says civilian casualties in Gaza are unacceptable
News broadcaster Al Jazeera claim that its office in Gaza is under attack by the Israeli Defence Force
Oldest Qur'an fragments are discovered in the collection of Birmingham University, with radiocarbon testing dating them to AD 568 - AD 645
A man shoots and kills nine people at the Olympia shopping mall in Munich, Germany, and then kills himself
Japan’s Funai Electric announces it will manufacture the world's last videocassette this month
10 die in over-heated tractor-trailer in San Antonio, Texas in human-trafficking incident
South African golfer Branden Grace records the lowest round for a men's major championship with a 62 in the 3rd round at the British Open, Royal Birkdale, England
Heatwave in Japan kills 11 in one day
Israeli military and allies evacuate over 400 White Helmet Syrian rescue workers from its border with Syria to Jordan
Lone gunman shooting kills 3 including gunman and injures 13 in Toronto, Canada
Seattle basketball guard Sue Bird plays her record 500th WNBA game as the Storm go down 87-74 to the Atlanta Dream; finishes her career with 508 games for Seattle
TV show "Supergirl" announces casting of first live-action transgender superhero, by transgender actor Nicole Maines as Nia Nal
Air strikes have killed at least 31 people in Maarat al-Numan, north-western Syria, with Russian planes supporting Syrian government being blamed
French submarine Minerve rediscovered off coast of Toulon, France, after disappearing in 1968 with loss of 52 crew
India's lunar mission, Chandrayaan-2 successfully takes off from Satish Dhawan Space Center, Sriharikota aiming to be fourth nation to soft-land on the moon
NFL Dallas Cowboys named most valuable sports team in the world worth $5 billion, MLB New York Yankees 2nd at $4.6 billion, Real Madrid 3rd with $4.2 billion according to Forbes
California passes New York total for COVID-19 cases (415,763 vs 415,094), though NY death toll much higher
US at “another pivotal point in this pandemic” says CDC Director Dr. Rochelle Walensky as the Delta variant becomes dominant
Droupadi Murmu is the first tribal politician to be elected President of India (head of state, without executive powers)
Hottest day on Earth: average global temperature of 17.15°C (62.87°F) surpasses record set the previous day, according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service
Israeli air strikes on southern city of Khan Younis in Gaza kill at least 70 people, after Israel ordered an evacuation of part of a designated humanitarian zone
Mudslides from heavy rains kills at least 229 people in Kencho Shacha Gozdi district, southern Ethiopia
Pope Innocent II is ambushed and taken captive by the forces ofRoger IIof Sicily at Galluccio while trying to invade the Kingdom of Sicily
Treaty of Frankfurt is signed betweenMaximilian Iof Austria and KingCharles VIIIof France
William I of Orangemoves from Antwerp to Delft
Foundation laid in Madrid for Buen Retiro Palace for KingPhilip IV
Alexander Mackenziereaches the Pacific Ocean, becoming the first European to complete a transcontinental crossing of Canada
Duke of Wellingtondefeats French at Battle of Salamanca, Spain
Smolny Cathedral in Saint Petersburg, Russia is consecrated, originally commissioned byElizabeth of Russia
US Civil War:Battle of Atlanta:General Sherman's Union side defeats Confederate troops under General Hood, with 8,449 Confederate and 3,641 US casualties
Washington Senators future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherWalter Johnsonbecomes 1st to reach 3,000 career strikeout milestone (en route to 3,508) with 5 K's in 3-1 win over Cleveland Indians
NY Yankees buy future Baseball Hall of Fame shortstopLeo Durocherfrom Hartford Senators (Eastern League)
Wiley Postcompletes the first solo flight around the world, taking 7 days and 19 hours
Outside Chicago's Biograph Theatre, "Public Enemy No. 1"John Dillingeris mortally wounded by FBI agents
US Senate rejectsFDR's proposal to enlarge Supreme Court
Dutch Prime minister Dirk Jan De Geer meetsAdolf Hitlerseeking peace talks
300,000 Jews from the Warsaw Ghetto are sent to Treblinka Extermination Camp
US forces led by GeneralGeorge S. Pattonliberate Palermo, Sicily

Menachem Begin's Zionist militant group Irgun bombs the King David Hotel in Jerusalem, the British administrative headquarters for Palestine, killing 91 people of various nationalities and injuring 46
Richard Nixonbecomes the first US Vice President to preside over a cabinet meeting
Benjamin Britten's "Missa Brevis" in D premieres at Westminster Cathedral in London
In their second clashSonny Listononce again KOsFloyd Pattersonin round 1 at the Convention Center, Las Vegas to retain the world heavyweight boxing title
Edward HeathsucceedsAlec Douglas-Homeas leader of the British Conservative party
Aretha Franklinis arrested for disorderly conduct following a traffic accident in Highland Park, Michigan; charges are later dropped
Spanish dictatorFrancisco Franco's choice ofPrince Juan Carlosas his successor is ratified by the Spanish Parliament
US House of Representatives votes to restore citizenship to GeneralRobert E. Lee
Stiff Records releases "My Aim Is True", the debut album by British rockersElvis Costello& The Attractions in the UK, produced by Nick Lowe
Turkish terrorist Mehmet Ali Agca sentenced to life imprisonment in a court in Rome, Italy
Polish Prime MinisterWojciech Jaruzelskilifts martial law after 19 months
Jeffrey Dahmerconfesses to killing 17 men in 1978
Colombian drug lordPablo Escobarescapes prison
New York YankeesDon Mattinglyhits his 200th HR
Former NFL running back, broadcaster and actorO.J. Simpsonpleads "Absolutely 100% Not Guilty" of murder
MLB Atlanta Braves pitcherGreg Madduxthrows a complete game with just 78 pitches in 4-1 wi n over the Cubs at Wrigley Field in Chicago
Mike Babcockis again named head coach of Team Canada, this time for the 2014 Winter Olympics (they would repeat as gold medalists)
Hillary Clintonannounces Virginia Senator Tim Kaine as her Vice Presidential running mate
US PresidentDonald Trumpthreatens Iran in an all-caps tweet of "consequences" in response to speech by Iranian PresidentHassan Rouhani
Marvel superhero film "Avengers: Endgame" becomes the world's highest-grossing film, overtaking "Avatar" and earning $2.9 billion
Pakistani Prime MinisterImran Khanmeets with US PresidentDonald Trumpat the White House in Washington, D.C.
Kim Kardashianasks for understanding of her husbandKanye West's struggles with his bipolar disorder on Instagram
Taylor Swiftconcert in Seattle shakes the ground so hard it registers as equivalent to a magnitude 2.3 earthquake and does the same the following night
Eminem's “The Death of Slim Shady (Coup de Grâce)” is his 11th album to reach #1, replacingTaylor Swift's "The Tortured Poets Department" after 12 weeks at the top
US Postal Service issues a commemorative postage stamp honoringAlex Trebek, long-time host of TV quiz show "Jeopardy!" in Culver City, California

First major anti-government protests take place across Ukraine since the beginning of the war after President VolodymyrZelenskyysigns a law placing two anti-corruption agencies under the control of the Prosecutor General
Hungarian army under John Hunyadi defeats the forces of OttomanSultan Mehmed IIand ends the Siege of Belgrade (Nándorfehérvár), halting Ottoman expansion in Europe for 70 years
Ed Wood's cult classic "Plan 9 from Outer Space," often called one of the worst films ever, premieres starring Gregory Walcott andBela Lugosi
Joan of England, Queen consort of Scotland as wife of Alexander II and daughter of King John of England, born in Gloucester, England (d. 1238)
